meaning: Consumer Price Index,消费者物价指数
examples: This year's rise, an average of 2.7 percent, may be a fraction lower than last year's, but it is still well above the official Consumer Price Index (CPI) measure of inflation.
The CPI was up six-tenths of a percent in October.
This would have big impact on CPI growth.
The core CPI, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, rose 0.2%, which matched expectations.
The Fortune 500, in turn, found that an increasing CPI made it easy to squeeze suppliers.
